info.title: Organization Rest Services
info.description: Rest Services for managing the Organization.

get.summary: Get plants under the specific organization
Get employees associated with the specific organization

post.summary: (Cloud Only) Move person or connect person as employee to a company

get.summary: Get businessunits under the specific organization
Get members associated with the specific organization

post.summary: Add exiting persons to an organization

get.summary: Get subsidiary under the specific organization
Get departments under the specific organization

updateAction.description: Specify the sub-resource update action. See 6W Web Service Principles: Data Object Updates

identifier.description: The unique object identifier when an object is represented by a Public Resource URI.

source.description: The object server URL when an object is represented by a Public Resource URI.

relativePath.description: The object resource path when an object is represented by a Public Resource URI.

csrf.description: CSRF object is required to be sent back for update operations and may be provided with read operations.

success.description: The response data when the request succeeds with 200 status code.
Reflects true to indicate successful request.

error-400.description: The response data when the request fails with 400 or 500 error.

error-401.description: The response data when the request fails with 400 or 500 error.

error-403.description: The response data when the request fails with 400 or 500 error.

error-500.description: The response data when the request fails with 400 or 500 error.

400.description: BAD REQUEST. The request could not be understood by the server due to malformed syntax. The client SHOULD NOT repeat the request without modifications.

401.description: UNAUTHORIZED. The request requires user authentication. The response MUST include a WWW-Authenticate header field containing a challenge applicable to the requested resource. The client MAY repeat the request with a suitable Authorization header field. If the request already included Authorization credentials, then the 401 response indicates that authorization has been refused for those credentials. If the 401 response contains the same challenge as the prior response, and the user agent has already attempted authentication at least once, then the user SHOULD be presented the entity that was given in the response, since that entity might include relevant diagnostic information. HTTP access authentication is explained in HTTP Authentication: Basic and Digest Access Authentication

500.description: INTERNAL SERVER ERROR. A generic error message, given when an unexpected condition was encountered and no more specific message is suitable.

SecurityContext.name: SecurityContext
SecurityContext.description: Security context the logged in user is using like ctx::RoleValue.OrganizationValue.CollaborativeSpaceValue

SecurityContextForPost.name: SecurityContext
SecurityContextForPost.description: Security context the logged in user is using like ctx::RoleValue.OrganizationValue.CollaborativeSpaceValue

Accept-Language.name: Accept-Language
Accept-Language.description: Identifies the locale for the web request.

ENO_CSRF_TOKEN.name: ENO_CSRF_TOKEN
ENO_CSRF_TOKEN.description: The ENOVIA CSRF token which is typically required for update operations.  Retrieve using {server}/resources/v1/application/CSRF

include.name: $include
include.description: Identify sub-resources that should be returned by the web request.

level.name: level
level.description: Number of nested levels., When value is 0, it will fetch all sub-organizations.

fields.name: $fields
fields.description: Identify resource and sub-resource fields that should be returned by the web request.
